policy.sameSegment: Beaches in the same curated shoreline segment should render the same visible exposure level unless a verified local morphology or wind-sport exception locks a stricter level.
policy.confidence: Segment propagation may support map-color consistency and medium review, but it never creates high confidence by itself.

segments.notes: Gero Aggeli, Plakotos, and Karatzas are neighboring northwest-facing north Ios records; name/duplicate risk and access confidence remain separately audited.
Vathy Rema, Roussou, Sofias Avlaki, and Psathi sit on the same practical northeast/east Ios coast; amenities and road access remain separate.
Almyros, Diammoudia, and Lorentzaina are nearby west-facing records with similar practical exposure behavior.
Valmas, Katsiveli, Kolitsani, and Mylopotas are adjacent southwest-facing Chora/Mylopotas records; resort popularity and access remain separate.
Koumpara and Tzamaria are nearby south/southwest-facing port-side records with similar map exposure behavior.
Klima, Pikri Nero, and Sapounochoma are neighboring southwest-facing remote records; difficult access must still block top-pick promotion.
Kalamos, Papas, and Plakes are nearby east/southeast Ios records with related shoreline behavior; boat/difficult access remains separate.
Louka Avlaki, Manganari, the Tris Klisies records, and Chalara sit on the broader southeast south-Ios beachfront system; access and individual cove confidence remain separate.
Santoriniko and Trypiti are neighboring south/southwest remote records; Antonarou is kept separate because east-wind exposure differs.
Manousou and Pepa are adjacent southwest-facing boat-only records and should render consistently while remaining blocked from mainstream top picks.
